overrides.new: \begin{array}{l} \text{When } t<0,\ a=\tau\dot{a}\Rightarrow a(t)=Ae^{t/\tau};\ \text{when } t>0,\ a=\tau\dot{a}\Rightarrow a(t)=Be^{t/\tau};\ \Delta a=B-A=-\frac{k}{m\tau} \\ \Rightarrow B=A-\frac{k}{m\tau},\ \text{so the general solution is } \boxed{a(t)=\left\{ \begin{array}{ll} Ae^{t/\tau}, & (t<0); \\ \left[A-(k/m\tau)\right]e^{t/\tau}, & (t>0). \end{array} \right.} \end{array}

overrides.expect: \begin{array}{l} \text {(a) From Prob. 11.34 we have \gamma = \frac {1}{b} \sqrt {b ^ {2} + c ^ {2} t ^ {2}} . \therefore \tau = \int \frac {1}{\gamma} dt = b \int \frac {dt}{\sqrt {b ^ {2} + c ^ {2} t ^ {2}}} = \frac {b}{c} \ln(ct+ \sqrt {b ^ {2} + c ^ {2} t ^ {2}}) + k ; a t} \\ t = 0 \text { we want \tau = 0: } 0 = \frac {b}{c} \ln b + k, \text { so } k = - \frac {b}{c} \ln b; \boxed {\tau = \frac {b}{c} \ln \left[ \frac {1}{b} (c t + \sqrt {b ^ {2} + c ^ {2} t ^ {2}}) \right]} \end{array}
overrides.new: \begin{array}{l} \text{(a) From Prob. 11.34 we have } \gamma=\frac{1}{b}\sqrt{b^{2}+c^{2}t^{2}}\text{. } \therefore\ \tau=\int \frac{1}{\gamma}\,dt=b\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt{b^{2}+c^{2}t^{2}}}=\frac{b}{c}\ln(ct+\sqrt{b^{2}+c^{2}t^{2}})+k;\ \text{at } t=0\text{ we want } \tau=0:\ 0=\frac{b}{c}\ln b+k,\ \text{so } k=-\frac{b}{c}\ln b;\ \boxed{\tau=\frac{b}{c}\ln\left[\frac{1}{b}(ct+\sqrt{b^{2}+c^{2}t^{2}})\right]} \end{array}
